1_ Translate the following legal text into colloquial Farsi 2_ Place the Persian and English text side by side in the table 3_ From the beginning to the end of the text, there should be an English sentence on the left side and a Persian sentence on the right side.
       4- Using legal language for Persian translation

          .Lesson Fifteen
Word Study
/
avenge ǝ'vend3 / to punish or hurt someone for a wrong done; to take or get
revenge.
He avenged his father's murder.
She avenged herself on her father's killers.
To avenge his father's death, he killed the killer's mother.
execution
eksı'kju:fn / capital punishment; the killing of someone as a legal
punishment.
Over 200 executions have been carried out this year.
The last federal execution in the US was in 1963.
The executions will serve as a warning to all criminals.
expert / 'ekspз:t/ a person with special knowledge, skill, or training in a particular
field.
A
person who gives expert advice in business or law is called a consultant.
A judge may refer the fact of a case to experts.
The defendant has to pay the expert fees and other litigation expenses incurred by the plaintiff.
fundamental fandǝ'mentl/basic; most important; essential; necessary.
You have to learn the fundamental rules of the road before you can get a licence.
There are fundamental differences between the penal systems before and after the revolution in Iran.
The most fundamental liberty in a democracy is freedom.
gruesome/ 'gru:səm / horrible; causing feelings of horror or disgust.
He did not alter his way of life, except to add two gruesome murders to his chain of crimes.

|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Lesson Fifteen                                                                                                                                                                                                | درس پانزدهم                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| Word Study                                                                                                                                                                                                    | مطالعه‌ی واژگان                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| avenge /ə'vendʒ/ to punish or hurt someone for a wrong done; to take or get revenge.                                                                                                                          | انتقام گرفتن / به مجازات کشاندن یا آسیبی به کسی رساندن به خاطر اشتباهی که انجام داده؛ گرفتن یا بدست آوردن انتقام.                                                                                    |
| He avenged his father's murder.                                                                                                                                                                               | او انتقام قتل پدرش را گرفت.                                                                                                                                                                                |
| She avenged herself on her father's killers.                                                                                                                                                                  | او از قاتلان پدرش انتقام خود را گرفت.                                                                                                                                                                       |
| To avenge his father's death, he killed the killer's mother.                                                                                                                                                  | برای انتقام جویی از مرگ پدرش، او مادر قاتل را کُشت.                                                                                                                                                          |
| execution /ˌeksɪˈkjuːʃn/ capital punishment; the killing of someone as a legal punishment.                                                                                                                    | اجرای حکم اعدام / مجازات اعدام؛ کشتن کسی به عنوان مجازات قانونی.                                                                                                                                             |
| Over 200 executions have been carried out this year.                                                                                                                                                          | بیش از 200 اجرای حکم اعدام در این سال انجام شده است.                                                                                                                                                         |
| The last federal execution in the US was in 1963.                                                                                                                                                             | آخرین اجرای حکم اعدام فدرال در ایالات متحده در سال 1963 بود.                                                                                                                                                 |
| The executions will serve as a warning to all criminals.                                                                                                                                                      | اجرای احکام اعدام به عنوان هشداری برای تمام مجرمان خواهد بود.                                                                                                                                                 |
| expert /ˈekspɜːrt/ a person with special knowledge, skill, or training in a particular field.                                                                                                                  | متخصص / فردی با دانش، مهارت، یا آموزش ویژه در یک حوزه‌ی خاص.                                                                                                                                                 |
| A person who gives expert advice in business or law is called a consultant.                                                                                                                                    | فردی که مشاوره‌های تخصصی در کسب‌وکار یا حقوق ارائه می‌دهد، مشاور نامیده می‌شود.                                                                                                                                |
| A judge may refer the fact of a case to experts.                                                                                                                                                               | قاضی ممکن است حقایق یک پرونده را به متخصصین ارجاع دهد.                                                                                                                                                         |
| The defendant has to pay the expert fees and other litigation expenses incurred by the plaintiff.                                                                                                              | متهم باید هزینه‌های متخصص و سایر هزینه‌های دعوایی را که توسط شاکی به وجود آمده پرداخت کند.                                                                                                                     |
| fundamental /ˌfʌndəˈmentl/ basic; most important; essential; necessary.                                                                                                                                        | اساسی / پایه‌ای؛ مهم‌ترین؛ ضروری؛ لازم.                                                                                                                                                                     |
| You have to learn the fundamental rules of the road before you can get a licence.                                                                                                                             | شما باید قوانین اساسی رانندگی را قبل از اینکه بتوانید گواهینامه بگیرید یاد بگیرید.                                                                                                                              |
| There are fundamental differences between the penal systems before and after the revolution in Iran.                                                                                                           | تفاوت‌های اساسی بین سیستم‌های مجازات قبل و بعد از انقلاب در ایران وجود دارد.                                                                                                                                   |
| The most fundamental liberty in a democracy is freedom.                                                                                                                                                        | مهم‌ترین آزادی اساسی در یک دموکراسی، آزادی است.                                                                                                                                                              |
| gruesome /ˈɡruːsəm/ horrible; causing feelings of horror or disgust.                                                                                                                                           | وحشتناک / هولناک؛ ایجاد کننده‌ی احساسات وحشت یا تنفر.                                                                                                                                                        |
| He did not alter his way of life, except to add two gruesome murders to his chain of crimes.                                                                                                                   | او روش زندگی خود را تغییر نداد، به جز اینکه دو قتل وحشتناک را به سلسله جرائم خود اضافه کرد.                                                                                                                    |

Ancient and modern, civil and gruesome, are the two reasons that account for the custom.
Often, very gruesome murder cases are the subject of great media interest. recurrence/ri'karǝns / the fact or process of something happening again; the state of accruing again.
We need to prevent a recurrence of the situation.
The court has taken all reasonable steps to prevent the recurrence of the offence.
The police acted promptly to end the violation and to prevent its recurrence. testimony 'testiməni / the statement of a witness under oath to tell the truth; evidence, especially given by a witness.
According to the witness's testimony, you were present when the crime was committed.
Clinton became the first president of the US to give sworn testimony to a criminal grand jury investigating his actions.
The deliberate giving of false testimony under oath is a crime. universally/ju:nı'vз:sǝli / by everyone; in every case; everywhere.
Many laws and regulations do not apply universally.
Our legal system should accept the universally recognised rules and regulations
of international law.
The principles of free consent and of good faith are universally recognised. utilise / 'ju:tǝlaiz / to make use of something; to put in use.
The law should provide economic incentives to induce producers to utilise innovative technology.
In Iran, the import licences are utilised for the importation of almost everything. There is an inherent right of all peoples to enjoy and utilise fully and freely
their natural wealth and resources.
violent 'vaiǝlǝnt / using, showing or caused by physical force that is intended to hurt or kill someone.
Alcohol makes them act in a violent and cruel way.
Students were involved in violent clashes with the police.
The increase in violent crimes is allied to the rise in unemployment.

| English Text                                                                                                                                                                     | Persian Text                                                                                                                                                                              |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Ancient and modern, civil and gruesome, are the two reasons that account for the custom.                                                                                          | باستانی و مدرن، متمدن و وحشتناک، دو دلیلی هستند که برای این رسم ذکر شده‌اند.                                                                                                                |
| Often, very gruesome murder cases are the subject of great media interest.                                                                                                       | بسیاری از اوقات، پرونده‌های قتل بسیار وحشتناک موضوع علاقه‌ی زیاد رسانه‌ها می‌شوند.                                                                                                          |
| recurrence /ri'kɜrəns/ the fact or process of something happening again; the state of accruing again.                                                                            | تکرار / حقیقت یا فرایندی که چیزی دوباره رخ می‌دهد؛ حالت رخ دادن مجدد.                                                                                                                      |
| We need to prevent a recurrence of the situation.                                                                                                                                 | ما باید از تکرار این وضعیت جلوگیری کنیم.                                                                                                                                                    |
| The court has taken all reasonable steps to prevent the recurrence of the offence.                                                                                               | دادگاه تمام گام‌های منطقی را برای جلوگیری از تکرار تخلف برداشته است.                                                                                                                         |
| The police acted promptly to end the violation and to prevent its recurrence.                                                                                                    | پلیس به سرعت عمل کرد تا نقض را پایان دهد و از تکرارش جلوگیری کند.                                                                                                                           |
| testimony /'testɪməni/ the statement of a witness under oath to tell the truth; evidence, especially given by a witness.                                                         | شهادت / بیانیه‌ای از شاهد تحت سوگند برای گفتن حقیقت؛ مدرک، به‌ویژه ارائه شده توسط شاهد.                                                                                                      |
| According to the witness's testimony, you were present when the crime was committed.                                                                                             | بر اساس شهادت شاهد، شما هنگام وقوع جرم حضور داشتید.                                                                                                                                         |
| Clinton became the first president of the US to give sworn testimony to a criminal grand jury investigating his actions.                                                        | کلینتون نخستین رئیس جمهور آمریکا شد که شهادت سوگندی به هیئت منصفه‌ی بزرگی که اقدامات او را تحقیق می‌کرد، ارائه داد.                                                                         |
| The deliberate giving of false testimony under oath is a crime.                                                                                                                 | ارائه‌ی عمدی شهادت کاذب تحت سوگند جرم است.                                                                                                                                                |
| universally /juːnɪˈvɜːrsəli/ by everyone; in every case; everywhere.                                                                                                              | به صورت جهانی / توسط هر کسی؛ در هر حالت؛ در همه جا.                                                                                                                                       |
| Many laws and regulations do not apply universally.                                                                                                                              | بسیاری از قوانین و مقررات به صورت جهانی اعمال نمی‌شوند.                                                                                                                                     |
| Our legal system should accept the universally recognised rules and regulations of international law.                                                                            | نظام حقوقی ما باید قوانین و مقررات به رسمیت شناخته شده‌ی جهانی حقوق بین‌الملل را بپذیرد.                                                                                                      |
| The principles of free consent and of good faith are universally recognised.                                                                                                     | اصول رضایت آزاد و حسن نیت به صورت جهانی به رسمیت شناخته شده‌اند.                                                                                                                         |
| utilise / 'juːtɪlaɪz / to make use of something; to put in use.                                                                                                                  | بهره‌برداری کردن / استفاده کردن از چیزی؛ به کار گرفتن.                                                                                                                                     |
| The law should provide economic incentives to induce producers to utilise innovative technology.                                                                                 | قانون باید مشوق‌های اقتصادی را فراهم کند تا تولیدکنندگان را به بهره‌برداری از تکنولوژی نوآورانه ترغیب کند.                                                                                    |
| In Iran, the import licences are utilised for the importation of almost everything.                                                                                              | در ایران، مجوزهای واردات برای واردات تقریباً همه چیز بهره‌برداری می‌شوند.                                                                                                                   |
| There is an inherent right of all peoples to enjoy and utilise fully and freely their natural wealth and resources.                                                              | حق ذاتی همه‌ی مردم برای لذت بردن و بهره‌برداری کامل و آزاد از ثروت‌ها و منابع طبیعی خود وجود دارد.                                                                                            |
| violent /'vaɪələnt/ using, showing or caused by physical force that is intended to hurt or kill someone.                                                                         | خشن / استفاده، نمایش یا ناشی از نیروی فیزیکی که قصد دارد به کسی آسیب برساند یا او را بکشد.                                                                                                    |
| Alcohol makes them act in a violent and cruel way.                                                                                                                               | الکل باعث می‌شود آن‌ها به شیوه‌ای خشن و ظالمانه رفتار کنند.                                                                                                                               |
| Students were involved in violent clashes with the police.                                                                                                                       | دانش‌آموزان در درگیری‌های خشن با پلیس دخیل بودند.                                                                                                                                         |
| The increase in violent crimes is allied to the rise in unemployment.                                                                                                            | افزایش جرائم خشونت‌آمیز با افزایش بیکاری مرتبط است.                                                                                                                                        |

Responsibility
One of the fundamental presumptions of the criminal law and criminal liability is that the defendant is able to function within normal range of mental and physical capabilities. So, it is universally agreed that persons suffering from serious mental disorders should be relieved of the consequences of their criminal conduct.
Insanity, in law, is a condition of mind that causes a person to be incapable of attending to his own affairs or not responsible for his conduct. A decision as to an individual's mental condition in a court proceeding may utilise expert testimony, and such testimony frequently produces conflicting medical and legal opinions. According to an English case, an insane person is excused only if he did not know the nature and quality of his act or could not tell right from wrong.
Although the law reads that a man guilty of a criminal act will not be punished if he was insane at the time of the act and will not be prosecuted if he has since become insane, opinion today is still clouded as to the purpose of criminal laws; whether they should protect society, punish, or avenge. If a violent, gruesome crime occurs, as in the murder of a child, society may demand that punishment be by execution, rather than by imprisonment, to avenge the child victim and make recurrences impossible. To accomplish these purposes, the accused must be found to have been sane at the time of the act.

| English Text                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Persian Text                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Responsibility                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | مسئولیت                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| One of the fundamental presumptions of the criminal law and criminal liability is that the defendant is able to function within normal range of mental and physical capabilities.                                                                   | یکی از فرض‌های اساسی قانون جنایی و مسئولیت جنایی این است که متهم قادر به عملکرد طبیعی در دامنه مهارت‌های ذهنی و جسمی باشد.                                                                                                                  |
| So, it is universally agreed that persons suffering from serious mental disorders should be relieved of the consequences of their criminal conduct.                                                                                                  | بنابراین، به طور جهانی مورد توافق است که اشخاصی که از اختلالات روانی جدی رنج می‌برند، باید از عواقب رفتارهای جنایی خود معاف شوند.                                                                                                               |
| Insanity, in law, is a condition of mind that causes a person to be incapable of attending to his own affairs or not responsible for his conduct.                                                                                                   | جنون، در حقوق، حالتی از ذهن است که باعث می‌شود فرد قادر به اداره امور خود نباشد یا مسئول رفتارهای خود نشود.                                                                                                                                     |
| A decision as to an individual's mental condition in a court proceeding may utilise expert testimony, and such testimony frequently produces conflicting medical and legal opinions.                                                              | تصمیم‌گیری در مورد وضعیت روانی فرد در جریان دادرسی دادگاه ممکن است از شهادت‌های متخصصین استفاده کند، و چنین شهادت‌هایی معمولاً نظرات پزشکی و حقوقی متضادی تولید می‌کند.                                                                           |
| According to an English case, an insane person is excused only if he did not know the nature and quality of his act or could not tell right from wrong.                                                                                             | بر طبق یک پرونده انگلیسی، فرد دیوانه فقط در صورتی معاف می‌شود که او ماهیت و کیفیت عمل خود را ندانسته باشد یا نتوانسته باشد درست و نادرست را تشخیص دهد.                                                                                         |
| Although the law reads that a man guilty of a criminal act will not be punished if he was insane at the time of the act and will not be prosecuted if he has since become insane, opinion today is still clouded as to the purpose of criminal laws; | هرچند قانون بیان می‌کند که مردی که مرتکب عمل جنایی شده است اگر در زمان ارتکاب عمل دیوانه بوده باشد مجازات نخواهد شد و در صورتی که پس از آن دیوانه شده باشد پیگرد قانونی نخواهد شد، نظر امروز هنوز در مورد هدف از قوانین جنایی مبهم است؛ |
| whether they should protect society, punish, or avenge.                                                                                                                                                                                           | خواه باید از جامعه محافظت کنند، مجازات نمایند، یا انتقام بگیرند.                                                                                                                                                                              |
| If a violent, gruesome crime occurs, as in the murder of a child, society may demand that punishment be by execution, rather than by imprisonment, to avenge the child victim and make recurrences impossible.                                     | اگر جرمی خشن و وحشتناکی رخ دهد، مثل قتل یک کودک، جامعه ممکن است تقاضا کند که مجازات از طریق اعدام باشد، تا از طریق زندانی کردن، تا قربانی کودک انتقام گرفته شود و تکرار آن غیرممکن شود.                    |
| To accomplish these purposes, the accused must be found to have been sane at the time of the act.                                                                                                                                               | برای دستیابی به این اهداف، باید تشخیص داده شود که متهم در زمان ارتکاب عمل عاقل بوده است.                                                                                                                                                           |

Lesson Sixteen
Word Study
acquit 'ǝkwit / to declare someone to be not guilty of a crime; to set free; to release.
The jury acquitted him of the charge of murder.
He was acquitted in default of strong evidence of his guilt.
The accused was acquitted of the charge.
aggravate/'ægrǝveit / to make a situation or an offence worse or more serious.
He aggravated his condition by lying under the oath.
The problem is aggravated by a lack of understanding.
An aggravated assault is an assault with intent to commit some additional
crime.
confirm/kǝn'fз:m/ to make certain or sure; to provide evidence that something is
true or correct.
The rumours of an attack were later confirmed.
His qualifications confirm his eligibility for being a judge.
I am writing to confirm your entitlement to bring an action against the seller. defect / 'di:fekt/ a fault; weakness; deficiency.
A mental defect relieves a person of criminal responsibility for his conduct.
The employer is liable to an employee injured by a defect in the equipment he
provides.
The immediate seller was found liable for damages caused by defects in goods. harsh ha:f/ungentle or unpleasant in effect; rough or sharp in action; severe; cruel.
Poor people are being bled dry by the country's harsh taxes. The judge dealt out harsh sentences to the convicted robbers.

| English Text                                                                                                                                                   | Persian Text                                                                                                                                                             |
|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Lesson Sixteen                                                                                                                                                 | درس شانزدهم                                                                                                                                                             |
| Word Study                                                                                                                                                    | مطالعه‌ی واژگان                                                                                                                                                        |
| acquit /ə'kwɪt/ to declare someone to be not guilty of a crime; to set free; to release.                                                                      | تبرئه کردن / اعلام بی‌گناهی شخصی نسبت به جرمی؛ آزاد کردن؛ رها کردن.                                                                                                       |
| The jury acquitted him of the charge of murder.                                                                                                               | هیئت منصفه او را از اتهام قتل تبرئه کرد.                                                                                                                                |
| He was acquitted in default of strong evidence of his guilt.                                                                                                  | او در نبود شواهد قوی از گناهکار بودنش تبرئه شد.                                                                                                                        |
| The accused was acquitted of the charge.                                                                                                                      | متهم از اتهام تبرئه شد.                                                                                                                                                 |
| aggravate /ˈæɡrəveɪt/ to make a situation or an offence worse or more serious.                                                                                | وخیم‌تر کردن / بدتر یا جدی‌تر کردن یک وضعیت یا جرمی.                                                                                                                    |
| He aggravated his condition by lying under the oath.                                                                                                          | او وضعیت خود را با دروغ گفتن زیر سوگند بدتر کرد.                                                                                                                       |
| The problem is aggravated by a lack of understanding.                                                                                                         | مشکل با کمبود درک و فهم بدتر می‌شود.                                                                                                                                    |
| An aggravated assault is an assault with intent to commit some additional crime.                                                                              | حمله با شدت، حمله‌ای است که با قصد ارتکاب جرم اضافی صورت می‌گیرد.                                                                                                        |
| confirm /kənˈfɜːm/ to make certain or sure; to provide evidence that something is true or correct.                                                             | تأیید کردن / قطعی یا مطمئن کردن؛ ارائه مدرکی که چیزی واقعی یا درست است.                                                                                                  |
| The rumours of an attack were later confirmed.                                                                                                                | شایعات حمله بعداً تأیید شد.                                                                                                                                             |
| His qualifications confirm his eligibility for being a judge.                                                                                                 | صلاحیت‌های او، صلاحیت او برای بودن به عنوان یک قاضی را تأیید می‌کند.                                                                                                      |
| I am writing to confirm your entitlement to bring an action against the seller.                                                                               | من می‌نویسم تا حق شما برای اقدام علیه فروشنده را تأیید کنم.                                                                                                              |
| defect / 'dɪˌfekt/ a fault; weakness; deficiency.                                                                                                             | عیب / نقص؛ ضعف؛ کمبود.                                                                                                                                                   |
| A mental defect relieves a person of criminal responsibility for his conduct.                                                                                 | یک نقص ذهنی، فرد را از مسئولیت جنایی برای رفتارش رها می‌کند.                                                                                                             |
| The employer is liable to an employee injured by a defect in the equipment he provides.                                                                       | کارفرما مسئول خسارت به کارمندی است که توسط عیب در تجهیزاتی که او فراهم می‌کند، آسیب دیده است.                                                                             |
| The immediate seller was found liable for damages caused by defects in goods.                                                                                 | فروشنده فوری برای خسارت‌های ناشی از عیوب کالا مسئول شناخته شد.                                                                                                            |
| harsh /hɑːʃ/ ungentle or unpleasant in effect; rough or sharp in action; severe; cruel.                                                                       | تند / خشن یا ناخوشایند در اثر؛ خشن یا تیز در عمل؛ سخت؛ ظالمانه.                                                                                                          |
| Poor people are being bled dry by the country's harsh taxes.                                                                                                 | مردم فقیر توسط مالیات‌های سنگین کشور خون‌شان ریخته می‌شود.                                                                                                               |
| The judge dealt out harsh sentences to the convicted robbers.                                                                                                 | قاضی مجازات‌های سختی را برای دزدان محکوم توزیع کرد.                                                                                                                       |

Where consecutive sentences are imposed, the combined sentence should not be unduly long or harsh.
incapacity,inkǝ'pæsəti / the lack of capacity; legal disqualification; incapability; inability.
The Parliament votes to oust President from office due to his mental incapacity. Judges may be removed for incapacity or misbehaviour.
The incapacity may be caused by insanity.
indictment / m'dartment / a formal accusation; the action of indicting somebody. A formal document accusing a person of committing an offence is called an indictment.
Spain issued a formal indictment to justify General Pinochet's extradition.
A document specifying the particulars of an offence of which a person is accused is an indictment.
intoxication/in, toksi'kein / drunkenness; the act of intoxicating.
In many states, a blood level of 0.10% is accepted as legal evidence of intoxication.
Special precautions must be taken to avoid the dangers of intoxication, addiction, and suicide.
If the mental disability is due to voluntary intoxication, he may not use it as a defence.
narcotic / na:'kútik / a drug that affects the mind; a substance which is often habit- forming.

Intoxication
Intoxication is usually not treated as mental incapacity. Research confirms that many of those who commit crimes of violence and burglary have taken some kind of intoxicant beforehand. Alcohol is probably the most widely used of intoxicant, but narcotic drugs are involved in some cases too. It is well known that people who have taken intoxicants tend to say or do things which they would not say or do when sober and in that sense, intoxicants may be regarded as the cause of certain behaviour.
When a person is so intoxicated that he is incapable of forming the mens rea required to be guilty of a particular crime, he is usually entitled to be acquitted if the crime is one that requires a specific intention but not if it requires a basic intention. Intoxication will not be a defence, however, if the crime is one of specific intent that can be committed by being reckless and the indictment is framed in terms of recklessness.
Nevertheless, Soviet law was especially harsh; it held that the mental-disease defence was not applicable to persons who committed a crime while drunk and that drunkenness might even be an aggravating circumstance. In German law, on the other hand, intoxication like any other mental defect is acceptable as a defence in criminal cases. In Iranian law, if a person drinks alcohol to commit a crime, he may be punished not only for the drinking alcohol but also for the crime committed as a result of intoxication.

Mitigating Circumstances and Other Defences
The law generally recognises a number of particular situations in which the use of force, even deadly force, is excused or justified. The draft Criminal Code of the English Law Commission has set forth six circumstances in which the use of force might be justified:
1. To prevent or terminate crime or to effect or assist in the lawful arrest of an offender or suspected offender.
2. To prevent or terminate a breach of the peace.
3. To protect himself or another from unlawful force or unlawful personal harm.
4. To prevent or terminate the unlawful detention of himself or another. 5. To protect property whether belonging to himself or another from unlawful appropriation, destruction, or damage.
6. To prevent to terminate a trespass to his property.
One of the most important bodies of law in this area is that which relates to self-defence. In general, in Anglo-American law, one may kill an assailant when the killer reasonably believes that he is in imminent peril of losing his life or of suffering serious bodily injury and that killing the assailant is necessary to avoid imminent peril. Other situations in which the use of force is generally justifiable include the use of force in defence of others, law enforcement, and protection of property.

Necessity
The use of force may also be excused if the defendant reasonably believed himself to be acting under necessity. The doctrine of necessity in Anglo-American law relates to situations in which a person, confronted by the over-whelming pressure of natural forces, must make a choice between evils and engages in conduct that would otherwise be considered criminal. In a well-known case a longboat containing passengers and members of the crew of a sunken American vessel was cast adrift in the stormy sea. To prevent the boat from being swamped, members of the crew threw some of the passengers overboard. In the trial of one of the crew members, the court recognised that such circumstances of necessity may constitute a defence to a charge of criminal homicide, provided that those sacrificed be fairly selected, as by lot. Because this had not been done, a conviction for manslaughter was returned.
In another case, however, two seamen who had been shipwrecked for nearly three weeks killed and ate the cabin boy, who was the only other survivor. The defence of necessity was rejected and they were found guilty of murder, even though the jury considered that they would have died from starvation if they had not killed the boy.
The use of force may also be excused if the defendant reasonably believed himself to be acting under duress or coercion, or to be carrying out military orders believed by the defendant to be lawful.
